args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
// Find assets in Hall4 via Hierarchical Structures
MATCH (hall4:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(rel:RelationshipElement {idShort: 'IsPartOf'})
MATCH (rel)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(ref:Reference)
RETURN ref.target AS asset_ref
LIMIT 50

MATCH (hall4:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id

// Explore what's inside the HierarchicalStructures submodel
MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.entityType
ORDER BY el.idShort

// Get detailed structure of entities in Hall4's hierarchical structures
MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entity:Entity {entityType: 'SelfManagedEntity'})
WHERE entity.idShort IN ['EntryNode', 'CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (entity)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:SubmodelElement)
RETURN entity.idShort AS entity_idShort, labels(entity) AS types, child.idShort AS child_idShort, labels(child) AS child_types
ORDER BY entity.idShort, child.idShort

// Get capability descriptions for each asset
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002', 'CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'MiR250_001', 'MiR250_002', 'UR20_001', 'UR3e_Type', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN aas.idShort AS asset_idShort, sm.idShort AS submodel_idShort
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

// Get the capability descriptions from type shells
MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(cap:Capability)
WHERE type.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'UR3e_Type']
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, cap.idShort AS capability_idShort
ORDER BY type.idShort, cap.idShort

MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
WHERE type.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'UR3e_Type']
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, sm.idShort AS submodel_idShort, el.idShort AS element_idShort, labels(el) AS element_types
ORDER BY type.idShort, sm.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sc.id AS semanticId, sm.idShort AS submodel_idShort
ORDER BY sc.id

MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(topEl:SubmodelElement)
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, topEl.idShort AS top_idShort, labels(topEl) AS top_types
ORDER BY type.idShort, topEl.idShort

MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(capSet:SubmodelElementCollection {idShort: 'CapabilitySet'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(cap:Capability)
WHERE type.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'UR3e_Type']
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, cap.idShort AS capability_idShort
ORDER BY type.idShort, cap.idShort

MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'CRX10iA_Type'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(capSet:SubmodelElementCollection {idShort: 'CapabilitySet'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:SubmodelElement)
RETURN child.idShort AS child_idShort, labels(child) AS child_types
ORDER BY child.idShort

// Drill into AssemblyContainer and HandlingContainer for CRX10iA_Type
MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'CRX10iA_Type'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(capSet:SubmodelElementCollection {idShort: 'CapabilitySet'})
MATCH (capSet)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(leaf:SubmodelElement)
WHERE leaf.modelType = 'Capability' OR leaf.idShort = 'Capability'
RETURN leaf.idShort AS capability_idShort, labels(leaf) AS types, capSet.idShort AS parent_idShort
ORDER BY capSet.idShort, leaf.idShort

// Let me see all elements inside the CapabilitySet for CRX10iA_Type
MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'CRX10iA_Type'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(capSet:SubmodelElementCollection {idShort: 'CapabilitySet'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort AS element_idShort, labels(el) AS types, el.entityType
ORDER BY el.idShort

// Get capabilities for all three type shells
MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'CapabilityDescription'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/SubmodelTemplate/CapabilityDescription/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(cap:Capability)
WHERE type.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'UR3e_Type']
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, cap.idShort AS capability_idShort
ORDER BY type.idShort, capability_idShort

// Get nameplate properties for the Hall4 instance shells
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/nameplate/3/0/Nameplate'})
WHERE aas.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002']
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(prop:Property)
RETURN aas.idShort AS asset_idShort, prop.idShort AS prop_idShort, prop.value AS prop_value, prop.valueType
ORDER BY aas.idShort, prop_idShort

// Get technical data properties for the type shells
MATCH (type:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'TechnicalData'})
WHERE type.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_Type', 'MiR100_Type', 'UR3e_Type']
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(prop:Property)
RETURN type.idShort AS type_idShort, prop.idShort AS prop_idShort, prop.value AS prop_value, prop.valueType
ORDER BY type.idShort, prop_idShort

// Check where UR3e_001 is located
MATCH (hall:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entity:Entity {idShort: 'UR3e_001'})
RETURN hall.idShort AS hall_idShort

// Check all halls and their contents
MATCH (hall:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(entity:Entity)
WHERE entity.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002', 'UR3e_001']
RETURN hall.idShort AS hall_idShort, entity.idShort AS entity_idShort
ORDER BY hall_idShort, entity_idShort

RETURN aas.idShort AS hallName, aas.id AS aasId, sm.id AS submodelId, lang.text AS primaryUse

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId

args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(node:Entity {idShort: 'Node'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (node)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(hasPart:RelationshipElement {idShort: 'HasPart'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (hasPart)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(ref:Reference)
RETURN node.idShort AS parentNode, hasPart.idShort AS relationType, ref.target_local AS deviceRef

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort AS elementIdShort, labels(el) AS labels, el.entityType AS entityType
LIMIT 30

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort AS entity, e.entityType, a.globalAssetId AS assetId, labels(e) AS typeLabels

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS deviceName, aas.id AS aasId, asset.globalAssetId AS assetId, asset.assetKind AS assetKind

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
WHERE aas.id IN ['urn:aas:crx10ia:001', 'urn:aas:mir100:001', 'urn:aas:ur3e:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS device, sm.idShort AS submodel, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'CRX10iA_001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS propType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'MiR100_001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS propType

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'UR3e_002'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS propType

result.response:
I'll help you find the devices in Hall 4. Let me start by getting the necessary schema and templates information, then query for the devices.

























I see a `FacilityInformation` template that likely contains hall/area data. Let me also check the cypher manual for anti-patterns, then query for halls and their contained devices.

Good — there are Hall 3 and Hall 4 facility AAS. Now let me look at the FacilityInformation submodel structure to understand how devices are listed, and also check if there's a container/hierarchical submodel for Hall 4.









The FacilityInformation template only has basic hall metadata (area, primary use, power). Devices are likely listed in a hierarchical container submodel. Let me check what other submodels Hall 4 has, and also look for HierarchicalStructures or similar container submodels.









There's a `HierarchicalStructures` submodel. Let me examine its structure and find the Entity tree that contains the devices.









Now let me query the HierarchicalStructures submodel to find the device entities. Devices are likely Entity nodes that have `REPRESENTS_ASSET` links to Asset nodes.









The graph normalized the suffix. Let me check what semanticIds are actually stored in the graph for the HierarchicalStructures submodel.









The semanticId matches. Let me try a broader traversal — maybe the Entities don't have REPRESENTS_ASSET links. Let me just list all Entities in that submodel.









Found 3 device entities (excluding EntryNode). Let me now get more details about each — their asset representation and any relationships.









Now let me get the AAS shells for these assets to see their nameplates for more details (manufacturer, model, etc.).









No nameplate submodels found. Let me check what submodels each device AAS has.









Each device has a Nameplate submodel. Let me query the properties within each nameplate.









Let me also check if there are any additional details about the device types — perhaps from the Entity nodes themselves or relationships to the AAS.









Let me check the relationships from these entities to understand how they link to the device AAS.









The entities appear to be leaf nodes in the hierarchical structure (they reference the AAS via the HasPart/SameAs relationships in the hierarchy, not via REPRESENTS_ASSET). Let me verify the hierarchy relationships.









Let me take a different approach and look at the HasPart/IsPartOf relationships.









Let me look at the structure more directly.









Now let me get the hall name properly and check the FacilityInformation submodel for additional details about Hall 4.









Let me also check for MultiLanguageProperty (PrimaryUse) which might need HAS_VALUE traversal.

















Now I have all the information. Let me compile the answer.

---

According to the graph data, **Hall 4** contains **3 devices**:

| Device | Asset ID | Serial Number | Manufactured |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CRX10iA_001** | `urn:asset:crx10ia:001` | CRX10IA-2024-001 | 2024-09-08 |
| **MiR100_001** | `urn:asset:mir100:001` | MIR100-2020-001 | 2020-04-12 |
| **UR3e_002** | `urn:asset:ur3e:002` | UR3E-2025-002 | 2025-01-18 |

**Hall 4 details** (from the FacilityInformation submodel):
- **Primary use:** Logistics
- **Area:** 12,000 m²
- **Power connection capacity:** 10 kW

The devices are listed in the hall's *HierarchicalStructures* submodel as Entity nodes, each managed by its own Asset Administration Shell with a Nameplate submodel containing the manufacturing details above.

tool_calls.name: get_manual_page
tool_calls.result_preview:
# Cypher patterns and anti-patterns

**Pre-condition.** Call `get_graph_schema()` (relationship labels) and
`get_templates_index()` (verbatim semanticIds) before composing any
Cypher. Invented labels

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm)
RETURN aas.idShort AS hallName, aas.id AS aasId, sm.idShort AS smIdShort, sm.id AS smId
LIMIT 20

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS templateSemanticId
ORDER BY sm.idShort

args.name: Hierarchical Structures enabling Bills of Material

args.cypher:
MATCH (container:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
      -[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort AS entity, asset.globalAssetId AS assetId, asset.assetKind AS kind
ORDER BY e.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
RETURN e.idShort AS entity, e.entityType AS type
ORDER BY e.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
WHERE e.idShort <> 'EntryNode'
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:HAS_SPECIFIC_ASSET_ID]->(sa:SpecificAssetId)
RETURN e.idShort AS device, asset.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId, asset.assetKind AS kind, 
       sa.name AS specificIdName, sa.value AS specificIdValue
ORDER BY e.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
OPTIONAL MATCH (aas)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/nameplate/3/0/Nameplate'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
WHERE p.idShort IN ['Manufacturer', 'ManufacturerProductName', 'Supplier', 'SupplierProductName']
RETURN aas.idShort AS deviceAas, asset.globalAssetId AS assetId, p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue
ORDER BY aas.idShort, p.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
MATCH (aas)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN aas.idShort AS device, sm.idShort AS submodel, sc.id AS templateSemanticId
ORDER BY aas.idShort, sm.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
MATCH (aas)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/nameplate/3/0/Nameplate'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN aas.idShort AS device, p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS vtype
ORDER BY aas.idShort, p.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
WHERE e.idShort IN ['CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002']
MATCH (e)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN e.idShort AS device, el.idShort AS element, el.modelType AS type, el.value AS val
ORDER BY e.idShort, el.idShort

MATCH (e:Entity {idShort: 'CRX10iA_001'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(child)
RETURN labels(child) AS childLabels, child.idShort AS childId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})
MATCH (e)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(n:Entity {idShort: 'Node'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (n)-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
RETURN n.idShort AS parentNode, child.idShort AS childNode, labels(child) AS labels

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(rel:RelationshipElement)
WHERE rel.idShort IN ['HasPart', 'IsPartOf', 'SameAs']
OPTIONAL MATCH (rel)-[:HAS_FIRST]->(first)
OPTIONAL MATCH (rel)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(second)
RETURN rel.idShort AS relType, labels(first) AS firstLabels, first.idShort AS firstId, labels(second) AS secondLabels, second.idShort AS secondId
ORDER BY relType, firstId

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})
MATCH path = (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*0..3]->(x:SubmodelElement)
WHERE x.idShort IN ['EntryNode', 'Node', 'CRX10iA_001', 'MiR100_001', 'UR3e_002']
RETURN x.idShort AS element, x.modelType AS type, labels(x) AS types
ORDER BY x.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN p.idShort AS propName, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS vtype

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(mlp:MultiLanguageProperty)
RETURN mlp.idShort AS propName

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(mlp:MultiLanguageProperty {idShort: 'PrimaryUse'})-[:HAS_VALUE]->(ls:LangString)
RETURN mlp.idShort AS propName, ls.language AS lang, ls.text AS text

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity)
WHERE parent.idShort = 'EntryNode'
MATCH (parent)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*0..]->(child:Entity)
MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort AS device, asset.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId, asset.assetKind AS kind
ORDER BY child.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN el.idShort AS elementIdShort, labels(el) AS types, count(*) AS cnt

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN e.idShort AS entity, labels(e) AS types, asset.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId, asset.assetKind AS kind
ORDER BY e.idShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
WHERE asset.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS deviceName, aas.id AS aasId, asset.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId, asset.assetKind AS kind
ORDER BY aas.idShort

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (child)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(asset:Asset)
RETURN child.idShort AS device, asset.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId
ORDER BY child.idShort

records.query: Which assets are in Hall 4?

result.query: Which assets are in Hall 4?
result.response:
I'll find which assets are located in Hall 4.

















I see a "Data Model for Asset Location" template — that's likely where hall information is stored. Let me also check for a hierarchical structures submodel (for hall→asset containment) and look at the FacilityInformation template since it mentions production halls.









The AssetLocation template tracks where assets move but doesn't seem ideal for static hall assignments. Let me also check the FacilityInformation template and the HierarchicalStructures template, which might define hall-to-asset containment.









The FacilityInformation template describes facility properties (area, use, power) but doesn't list contained assets. Let me check the HierarchicalStructures template which might define hall→asset containment relationships.









Good — HierarchicalStructures uses `HasPart` / `IsPartOf` RelationshipElements to express containment. Let me now query for assets in Hall 4. I'll approach this in two ways: (1) find facility submodels mentioning "Hall 4", then traverse `HasPart` relationships to find contained assets, and (2) look for assets with location properties indicating Hall 4.









I found a Hall 4 facility submodel (`urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation`). Now let me find which AAS contains this submodel, then traverse its hierarchical structure to find assets in Hall 4.









Now let me check if this AAS has a HierarchicalStructures submodel and traverse the `HasPart` relationships to find the assets it contains.









The Hall 4 AAS has a HierarchicalStructures submodel. Let me now traverse the `HasPart` relationship elements to find the contained assets.









The `HasPart` might be nested inside an Entity. Let me explore the structure of the HierarchicalStructures submodel more carefully.









I see Entity nodes for `CRX10iA_001`, `MiR100_001`, and `UR3e_002` inside the HierarchicalStructures. These look like asset references. Let me check if these Entities have `REPRESENTS_ASSET` links or if they reference other AAS shells.









Now let me also check the EntryNode's children to understand the hierarchy, and get the asset details for each.









Now let me get the AAS details for each of these assets to provide meaningful names.









Let me also get the nameplate info for each to provide better descriptions.

















Let me check what properties are actually in the nameplate submodels.









I have all the information needed. Here's the answer:

---

**Hall 4** (facility submodel: `urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation`, primary use: Logistics) contains **3 assets**, as defined in the Hierarchical Structures submodel:

| Asset | AAS ID Short | Serial Number | Year |
|---|---|---|---|
| **CRX10iA_001** | `CRX10iA_001` | `CRX10IA-2024-001` | 2024 |
| **MiR100_001** | `MiR100_001` | `MIR100-2020-001` | 2020 |
| **UR3e_002** | `UR3e_002` | `UR3E-2025-002` | 2025 |

These are the three instance shells nested under the `EntryNode` entity in the Hall 4 AAS's Hierarchical Structures submodel. The hall's primary use is listed as **Logistics**.

According to the graph data (HierarchicalStructures template, `HasPart` relationships via Entity nodes with `REPRESENTS_ASSET` links).

args.cypher:
MATCH (sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(mlp:MultiLanguageProperty {idShort: 'PrimaryUse'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (mlp)-[:HAS_VALUE]->(ls:LangString)
RETURN sm.id AS submodelId, sm.idShort, ls.text AS primaryUse

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:facilityinformation'})
RETURN aas.id AS aasId, aas.idShort AS aasIdShort

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)
OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept)
RETURN sm.idShort, sm.id, sc.id AS semanticId

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})
      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(hasPart:RelationshipElement {idShort: 'HasPart'})
MATCH (hasPart)-[:HAS_SECOND]->(ref:Reference)
RETURN ref.target_id AS assetRef

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(el:SubmodelElement)
RETURN labels(el) AS types, el.idShort, el.modelType
LIMIT 30

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(e:Entity)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:REPRESENTS_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
OPTIONAL MATCH (e)-[:HAS_SPECIFIC_ASSET_ID]->(sai:SpecificAssetId)
RETURN e.idShort AS entityName, a.globalAssetId AS assetGlobalId, sai.name AS saiName, sai.value AS saiValue

MATCH (sm:Submodel {id: 'urn:submodel:hall4:hierarchicalstructures'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(parent:Entity {idShort: 'EntryNode'})-[:HAS_ELEMENT]->(child:Entity)
RETURN child.idShort AS childName

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:MANAGES_ASSET]->(a:Asset)
WHERE a.globalAssetId IN ['urn:asset:crx10ia:001', 'urn:asset:mir100:001', 'urn:asset:ur3e:002']
RETURN aas.idShort AS assetIdShort, aas.id AS aasId, a.globalAssetId AS globalAssetId, a.assetKind

result.response:
I'll help you find which assets are in Hall 4. Let me first check the graph structure to understand how locations and assets are related.

















I see there's a "FacilityInformation" template for production hall data. Let me first find which AAS represents Hall 4, then discover what assets it manages.

**Expected structure:** I expect to find a FacilityInformation submodel with a property identifying the hall name/location, and the AAS that manages the asset(s) in that hall.

Let me query for the Hall 4 facility information:

















Now let me first check the FacilityInformation template structure, then find the Hall 4 submodel:









Good. The FacilityInformation submodel has `Area`, `PrimaryUse`, and `PowerConnection`. But I also need to understand how assets are associated with halls. Let me first find all submodels with the FacilityInformation semanticId and see which one represents Hall 4:









I found Hall 4's AAS. The `PrimaryUse` property returned null — let me check if it's a MultiLanguageProperty and retrieve it properly. Also, I need to find which assets are in Hall 4. Let me check the structure more carefully:









I can see there's a `PrimaryUse` MultiLanguageProperty. Let me retrieve its value, and also check what entities (assets) are in Hall 4:









Good, Hall 4's primary use is "Logistics". Now let me find which assets are managed by or associated with Hall 4. I see Entity nodes like `CRX10iA_001`, `MiR100_001`, `UR3e_002` — these might represent the assets. Let me check if they have `REPRESENTS_ASSET` links:









I found 4 entities in Hall 4. The `EntryNode` represents the hall itself. The actual assets are the other 3. Let me get more details about each asset — I'll look up their individual AAS shells to get their idShort (human-readable names):









Let me also check if there's a location submodel (AssetLocation template) that might give more descriptive location info for these assets:









No AssetLocation submodels found. Let me verify the results by checking the Hall 4 AAS's managed asset directly:









The Hall 4 AAS directly manages the hall itself as an asset. The actual equipment assets (robots, mobile robots) are represented via Entity nodes within the FacilityInformation submodel's Entity tree.

Let me also check if any of those asset AAS shells have DERIVED_FROM relationships to type shells for more detail:









Let me also check what submodels these asset shells have to understand what type of equipment they are:









Now let me get the nameplate details for each of the 3 assets in Hall 4 to provide meaningful descriptions:









Let me check the actual structure of the Nameplate submodel for these assets:

















Let me check all submodel elements in the Nameplate submodel more carefully:









Let me try a different approach — get the Property values directly:









Now let me get the same for the other two assets:









Let me also check the TechnicalDataAGV submodel for the MiR100 to understand what type of asset it is:









Now I have a clear picture. Let me also quickly check if there's a HierarchicalStructures submodel in Hall 4 that might list contained assets:









Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the answer.

---

**According to the graph data, Hall 4 (a logistics facility) contains the following 3 assets:**

| Asset | idShort | Serial Number | Year Built | Asset Type |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CRX10iA_001 | CRX10iA_001 | CRX10IA-2024-001 | 2024 | Industrial robot (FANUC CRX10iA) |
| MiR100_001 | MiR100_001 | MIR100-2020-001 | 2020 | Mobile robot / AGV (MiR100) |
| UR3e_002 | UR3e_002 | UR3E-2025-002 | 2025 | Collaborative robot (Universal Robots UR3e) |

**Details:**
- **CRX10iA_001** — FANUC CRX10iA collaborative robot, manufactured 2024-09-08
- **MiR100_001** — Mobile Industrial Robots MiR100 AGV, manufactured 2020-04-12
- **UR3e_002** — Universal Robots UR3e cobot, manufactured 2025-01-18

These assets are represented as Entity nodes within the Hall 4 FacilityInformation submodel, each with a `REPRESENTS_ASSET` link to their respective Asset node. The Hall 4 AAS also directly manages the hall itself as an asset (`urn:asset:hall4`).

OPTIONAL MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property {idShort: 'PrimaryUse'})
OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:HAS_VALUE]->(lang:LangString)

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)

      -[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property {idShort: 'ModelName'})
RETURN p.value AS modelName

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell {id: 'urn:aas:crx10ia:001'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel {idShort: 'Nameplate'})

Right, I need to avoid CONTAINS on IDs. Let me use the submodel_id directly since I already found it.









Found the Hall 4 AAS. Now let me explore what submodels and assets are associated with it, particularly looking for the HierarchicalStructures template which might contain the asset hierarchy.









The HierarchicalStructures submodel should contain the asset hierarchy. Let me explore its elements.









Good, I can see entities in the hierarchy. Now let me find which of these entities represent actual assets by checking for REPRESENTS_ASSET relationships.









I found the assets. Let me also check the hierarchical relationships between these entities to understand which are children of which (i.e., which assets belong to Hall 4 vs. are the hall itself).









Now let me get the AAS details for each of these asset entities to provide more meaningful names/descriptions.

RETURN aas.idShort AS assetIdShort, aas.id AS assetId, aas.idShort AS aasIdShort, hallVal.text AS hallName

MATCH (aas:AssetAdministrationShell)-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/dfki/SubmodelTemplate/FacilityInformation/1/0'})
MATCH (sm)-[:HAS_ELEMENT*]->(p:Property)
RETURN aas.idShort AS hallAas, p.idShort AS propIdShort, p.value AS propValue, p.valueType AS propValueType

MATCH (hall4:AssetAdministrationShell {idShort: 'Hall4'})-[:HAS_SUBMODEL]->(sm:Submodel)-[:HAS_SEMANTIC_ID]->(sc:SemanticConcept {id: 'https://admin-shell.io/idta/HierarchicalStructures/1/1/Submodel'})
